commit.message:
feat: add consumer build system with checked-in generated artifacts

Key distinction from other Muscovite sample projects: generated code is
committed to the repo (ADR-004). muscomp is a dev-time tool only — the
project builds with just C++ toolchain + Conan, no muscomp needed.

Added:
- CMakeLists.txt: Pure consumer build, finds muscovite via Conan/env/FetchContent
- conanfile.py: Depends on muscovite/2.23.0 package + direct deps
- CMakePresets.json: debug/release presets matching muscovite conventions
- conan_profiles/: debug + release profiles (C++23, Ninja, GCC)
- cmake/Version.cmake + version.hpp.in: Build provenance
- src/main.cpp: Bootstrap server entry point
- tests/harbor_smoke_test.cpp: BDD smoke test (doctest)
- tools/regenerate.sh: Dev-time muscomp invocation
- tools/verify-generated.sh: CI freshness check
- MUSCOMP_VERSION: Tracks which muscomp produced generated/
- HARBOR_VERSION: Project version
- ADR-004: Generated Artifacts Are Checked In

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement otela-fleet project with CLI, templates, and deployment (#54)

* feat(fleet): scaffold otela-fleet project in contrib/

* feat(fleet): add ClusterConfig with Preset dataclass and job_identity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(fleet): add fleet apply with new deployment format and job identity hashing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(fleet): add otela-fleet CLI with start/stop/status/logs/apply commands

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(fleet): port connection tests and EDF template

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(fleet): address code review findings

- Fix double #SBATCH prefix in extra_sbatch template rendering
- Use unique job script filenames to avoid race condition with replicas
- Add job_identity unit test
- Update template test to match extra_sbatch convention

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(fleet): add docs, clusters/presets commands, and config dir lookup

- Add README.md and docs/ (getting-started, cluster-config, fleet-apply)
- Add `otela-fleet clusters` and `otela-fleet presets <cluster>` commands
- Default config dir: ./clusters or ~/.config/opentela/fleet/clusters/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(fleet): add Euler, JSC, and Clariden cluster config examples

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(fleet): update Clariden example with long-running relay node

Clariden now has a persistent relay at 148.187.108.172, so relay
startup is skipped and workers bootstrap directly from it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: sort SLURM job IDs numerically when selecting excess jobs to cancel

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/eth-easl/OpenTela/sessions/12e7506b-7a54-4197-80a3-da0be219e533

Co-authored-by: xzyaoi <10499297+xzyaoi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Docker and CI/CD configurations, remove deprecated scripts, and enhance logging

- Updated GitHub Actions workflow to use new Dockerfile for amd64 architecture and set build metadata.
- Added new Dockerfile for building signed OpenTela binaries.
- Removed obsolete deployment scripts and configuration files for Clariden and Euler.
- Updated Dockerfile to streamline the build process and ensure proper entry points.
- Enhanced logging in the protocol to provide more detailed debug information.
- Adjusted public address handling to support DNS hostnames in the network configuration.

* baremetal docker serving docs

* feat(fleet_manager): add proxychains support + migrate cluster configs

Add ProxyChains dataclass to cluster.py with enabled/ssh_key/proxy_target/
socks_port/skip_partitions fields. When enabled and the preset partition is
not in skip_partitions, apptainer_single/multi templates open an SSH SOCKS
tunnel from inside the SLURM allocation and export HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY/
ALL_PROXY so downstream commands (apptainer pull, HF hub, user code) have
internet access via the tunnel.

Populate contrib/fleet_manager/clusters/ with jsc, jsc-dev, euler, clariden
migrated to the preset-based schema. JSC enables proxychains by default with
develbooster and dc-gpu-devel listed in skip_partitions.

Update README to document presets, user-command env vars ($SERVICE_PORT,
$HF_HOME), multi-node conventions, and proxychains. Delete stale docs/
files that described the old model-centric layout.

52/52 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update deploy playbook

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TE prompt issues: selector hallucination, duplicate files, assertion mismatch, timeouts

- Replace Blazor-specific selector blacklist with general 'only use selectors from source files'
- Update per-locator timeout from 30000ms to 60000ms across all prompt locations
- Add 'DO NOT CREATE DUPLICATE FILES' rule to system prompts and build-fix loops
- Add 'ASSERTIONS MUST MATCH SOURCE CODE, not spec documents' rule
- Fix design reference prompt to clarify it's informational only
- Make build-fix loop prompts tech-stack-agnostic (dependency manifest vs .csproj)
- Add code-level filter to block entry-point files (Program.cs, Startup.cs, etc.)

commit.message:
fix(ClosingQuoteView): use canonical URL for social share links

LinkedIn and Facebook only support passing a URL to their share dialogs
(text pre-fill is not supported). Both platforms pull title, description,
and image from the page's OG tags.

Replace window.location.href with the hardcoded canonical URL so shares
always point to the production page and OG tags are reliably scraped.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace invisible tap zone with visible toggle button

Small floating button in top-right corner  shows fire emoji
when on FlightView, plane emoji when on Kiln. Single tap to toggle.
Much more reliable than invisible double-tap zones on 7in screen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix gap below reply button when reply box is collapsed

Collapsed .reply-row had flex: 0 0 100% which claimed its own flex
line; the container's row-gap then added 6px of blank space after the
buttons even when the reply box was hidden.

Setting flex: 0 0 0% when closed removes it from the flow entirely.
flex: 0 0 100% is restored when the .open class is toggled on.

commit.message:
Fix skill activation: SessionStart hook + @federation agent

Problem: Squad's coordinator agent (squad.agent.md) intercepts all user
messages before plugin skills activate. 'Set up a team organization'
triggers Squad's Init Mode, not our federation-setup skill.

Solution (combined approach):
- Option A: SessionStart hook injects federation awareness into the
  coordinator's context. Lists specific topics to delegate to federation
  skills instead of handling as normal Squad init. Works immediately.
- Option B variant: @federation agent for explicit invocation. User can
  say '@federation set up a team organization' to bypass the coordinator
  entirely. Always available, no competition.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: robustify RptRequirementHandler JSON parsing (#225)

- Dispose JsonDocument with 'using' to release pooled memory promptly
- Use TryGetProperty for 'permissions', 'rsname', and 'scopes' so
  incomplete or non-standard RPT payloads skip gracefully instead
  of throwing KeyNotFoundException at runtime
- Add RptRequirementHandlerTests covering all new code paths

above iframes to reliably capture touch/click events.

Add multilingual dictionary OCR pipeline (Roerich) (#1)

* Add multi-model OCR comparison tools for dictionary digitization

- extract_pages.py: Extract pages from dictionary PDFs as high-res images
- test_ocr.py: Compare Gemini, GPT-4o, Claude, and Surya OCR on same pages
- requirements.txt: Dependencies for all supported models
- README.md: Setup and usage instructions
- .env.example: API key template
- .gitignore: Exclude keys and test outputs

Targets Roerich, Chandra Das, and Jaschke Tibetan dictionaries
with 4 scripts per page (Tibetan, English, Russian, Sanskrit).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add venv setup to README instructions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use structured JSON extraction only, upgrade to Gemini 3.1 Pro

- Remove raw OCR mode, always extract structured dictionary entries as JSON
- Update Gemini model from 2.5 to gemini-3.1-pro-preview
- Output .json files instead of .txt
- Remove --structured and --compare-only flags (structured is now the default)
- Update README accordingly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Migrate to google-genai SDK, strip markdown fences from output

- Replace deprecated google.generativeai with google.genai Client API
- Strip markdown code fences from JSON responses before saving
- Update requirements.txt: google-genai replaces google-generativeai
- Add roerich/ and .venv/ to .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add cross-page entry merging with fragment detection

- Update OCR prompt to flag split entries: continues_next_page and
  continued_from_prev_page flags on boundary entries
- Add merge_pages.py: combines sequential page JSONs, merging split entries
- Heuristic fallback: detects truncation via unmatched parens (ignoring
  numbered lists like 1), 2)), missing punctuation, absent headwords
- Tested on pages 2-3 of 1Ka: correctly detects truncated entries and
  avoids false positives on numbered definitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add idempotent Roerich OCR pipeline script

- ocr_roerich.py: processes PDF volumes into roerich_output/pages/ and
  roerich_output/json/ directories
- Idempotent: skips pages with existing JSON output unless --force
- Validates JSON output, saves raw response on parse errors for debugging
- Supports --skip-pages, --dpi, --force flags
- Can process single or multiple volumes (glob-friendly)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove ROERICH.zip from tracking (exceeds GitHub 100MB limit)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent zombie downloads from untracked transfers in previous runs

Three changes to download.py:

1. Pre-run stale cleanup (defense-in-depth)
   Before enqueueing, cancel any Queued/Paused/Errored/Cancelled leftovers for
   the same filenames from a prior failed run (include_active=False preserves
   concurrent InProgress downloads).  existing_ids is snapshotted first so stale
   IDs are naturally excluded from the post-enqueue diff regardless of slskd
   removal timing.

2. Abort-path untracked sweep (primary fix)
   All three abort paths (speed-check, queue-timeout, inactivity-timeout) now
   also call _cancel_transfers_for_filenames with skip_ids=active.values().
   This catches transfers that were enqueued but whose ID was never discovered
   by _find_new_transfer_id — those were silently left in slskd's queue and
   could resume downloading later as 'zombie' transfers.
   _cleanup_incomplete_files now receives both tracked and untracked filenames.

3. Smarter fallback ID resolution
   The filename-based fallback now prefers transfer IDs not in existing_ids
   (newly created) before falling back to the last-by-position entry, reducing
   the chance of latching onto a stale transfer from a previous run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: generated spec uses keyboard.type for contenteditable, waitForReady after project select

Three issues fixed in the generated Playwright spec:
1. page.fill() doesn't work with contenteditable spans — use
   keyboard.type() with delay instead (looks more natural too)
2. After project select or new session, must wait for 'Ready' status
   before interacting — session reinitializes with new bridge
3. Exact folder name match instead of fuzzy regex

Also: removed 2 stale duplicate specs, updated prompt template with
these patterns for future generations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Render conversation threads from data.messages

When a notification carries data.messages, display a rich
conversation thread instead of the plain body text:

- Each message shows a rounded bubble, sender name and avatar
  (or initials fallback) on the first message in each group
- Consecutive messages from the same sender are visually grouped:
  no repeated avatar/name after the first in each run
- Self-messages (no sender field) are right-aligned in green;
  others are left-aligned in the card's border colour
- senderIcon may be raw base64, a data URI, or a URL — all handled
- Plain body rendering is unchanged when data.messages is absent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct nameof() references in DecisionRequirementHandler (#241)

PR #228 fixed incorrect nameof() usage in RealmAccessRequirementHandler,
ResourceAccessRequirementHandler, and RptRequirementHandler, but missed
the same issue in DecisionRequirementHandler.

The handler was logging and recording metrics using
ParameterizedProtectedResourceRequirement/Handler names instead of its
own DecisionRequirement/Handler names, making logs misleading and metrics
incorrect.

commit.message:
fix: split release workflow into PR-based two-workflow pipeline

The original release.yml pushed directly to main, which fails due to
branch protection rules (GH013). Split into two workflows:

- release.yml: workflow_dispatch creates release/vX.Y.Z branch with
  version bump + changelog update, opens PR to main
- release-publish.yml: on merged release PR, creates tag + GitHub
  Release + deletes release branch

Also includes:
- Pin checkout to main in release.yml to prevent running from wrong branch
- Use merge_commit_sha in release-publish.yml to prevent race conditions
- Add semver validation in publish workflow
- Update CHANGELOG to reflect full scope of [Unreleased] additions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ci-coach] ci: run existing Gridlock Arena tests in JS and Python jobs (#9)

* ci: run existing Gridlock Arena tests in JS and Python jobs

Both Solutions/JavaScript/The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos/ and
Solutions/Python/ contain self-contained test suites that were never
executed by CI — only syntax-checked. This change:

- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-javascript job:
  runs node The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos.test.js (no npm install needed)
- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-python job:
  runs python test_gridlock_arena.py (no pip install needed)
- Fixes Python validation to use find instead of shell glob for
  consistency with the JS job and to cover future subdirectories

All tests verified passing locally before this commit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: strip markdown code fences when saving generated specs

The response from Copilot wraps the spec in ```typescript ... ```
fences. The save handler now extracts the content from inside the fence
before writing to disk, so the saved .spec.ts is valid TypeScript.

commit.message:
Fix(integrations): Guard invalid HA timezone

Handle null, empty, and invalid timezone strings
from the HA config endpoint.  HAClient.get_timezone
now validates the value is a non-empty string
before returning it.  RentalControlService wraps
ZoneInfo construction in a try/except to fall back
to UTC when the string is not a valid IANA zone.

fix(ViewTransition): enable vertical scrolling on small screens

- Active layer uses position:relative so content defines document height
- Enter/exit layers use position:absolute (top/left/right, no bottom) with
  pointerEvents:none so they animate without affecting layout
- Container uses overflow-x:hidden only, freeing vertical scroll
- Reset window scroll to top on each view change

which holds the full accumulated response after finishResponse().

commit.message:
fix: include album directory in completed file paths for organiser

slskd stores completed downloads as <completed_dir>/<album_dir>/<filename>
but the path was constructed using only the filename component, causing
'Source file not found' warnings for every download.

Use parts[-2]/parts[-1] (same pattern as _cleanup_incomplete_files) to
include the album directory when resolving remote paths to local paths.
